sections.paragraph:
ἐπάλληλος, ον pert. to someth. happening again and again, in rapid succession (Polyb., Diod S Plut. et al.; PFamTebt 15, 68 and 90) esp. of misfortunes (Plut., Pomp. 632 [25, 10]; Philo, Mos. 2, 263, In Flacc. 121 τὰς συνεχεῖς κ. ἐπαλλήλους κακώσεις; Jos., Bell. 5, 32) happening repeatedly ἐ. γενόμεναι συμφοραί 1 Cl 1:1.—DELG s.v. ἄλλος.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

someth. someth. = something

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

esp. esp. = especially

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 358
